notes: Measure Word: ê. From Mandarin "che-cheh". Speaker is the 4th girl; calls her older sisters tōa-ché, jī-ché, saⁿ-ché.

english: to give the change due on a purchase

notes: Measure Word: ê.
Can also mean within the territorial boundaries of the city.

english: outside the city / a quieter area away from downtown
downtown shopping area of a town or village
notes: The similar area of a city, formerly called ke-á, is now generally called chhī-lāi.

english: to feed / raise (animals) / support (family) / keep (a pet)

notes: Measure Word: ê. Lit. older sisters – younger sisters.

english: measure word for animals, birds, and boats (sometimes for tables/chairs)
(of a situation or person) bad / no good
notes: Synonym of pháiⁿ in this sense.
Often synonymous with mâ-hôan.
Negative: m̄-káⁿ.

english: cloth used for a particular purpose
notes: Measure Word: tiâu. Does not mean the material clothes are made of.
A square cloth kept in a purse/pocket, used dry mostly to wipe the face (esp. sweat); also to wipe the hands.

english: washcloth for the face (lit. face cloth)
notes: Used wet to clean the face.

english: washcloth for the body (lit. wash-body cloth)
notes: Used wet to wash the body and squeezed dry to dry off. bīn-kin and sé-seng-khu-kin are the same kind of cloth, distinguished only by use.
Measure Word: ki.
When mô· means wool it is always 5th tone; properly iûⁿ-mo· = the fleece of sheep. The hair of other animals is not necessarily 5th tone.
Measure Word: chiah.

english: to turn (of a person/animal/vehicle in motion)
by the side of / a side
over there / the other side / the other party

notes: Measure Word: ê.

english: to be acquainted with / familiar with (a person)

notes: Measure Word: ê. From Mandarin "me-meh".

english: to think / consider; think of / about
notes: siūⁿ + Negative = "I don't think that..."
Measure Word: ê.
Of arms/legs/things/life; not used of a person's height. Opposite of tn̂g.
Measure Word: tè/lia̍p.
Negative: m̄-thang. Can express morality or legality, esp. between superior and inferior; interchangeable with but less common than ē-sái-tit/bē-sái-tit (Ls 14) and mài (Ls 10).

english: head of a shop or firm; husband
notes: Also called láu-pán (from Mandarin). Younger couples say sian-siⁿ for husband.
Measure Word: lia̍p. thâu-khak always means the head of the body; thâu has other uses studied later.

english: to wash the hair / get a shampoo

notes: Orig. meaning of thì is to shave the head.

english: to get a haircut / to give a haircut

notes: Measure Word: keng.

english: to open / operate a shop for business
in the middle of / between / during / in the course of

notes: Opposite of té.
